mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 0.50% signals monetary tightening that increases the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.50% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2021-11-15 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 0.50% for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling labor market weakening that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where PAYEMS growth < 100k for 2+ months, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth decelerates as measured by RSAFS, combined with housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,400 thousand units annualized, signals demand-side weakness that reduces corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below 0.2% AND HOUST falls below 1,400 thousand units in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 639,829 million and HOUST at 1,520 thousand as of 2021-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances where both RSAFS growth < 0.2% and HOUST < 1,400k, or joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.3% month-over-month, signaling supply-side constraints and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization that compress profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below 0.3% for 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 52% of instances where INDPRO growth < 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.30 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.30pp over any 3-month rolling window
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.30pp over 3 months, or Spearman rank correlation between UNRATE change and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 5.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating a negative real interest rate regime that forces the Federal Reserve into tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate policy reversal.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 5.0% AND FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 278.88 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2021-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL YoY > 5.0% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% in any quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,469.398 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2021-10-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,600 thousand units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) maintain month-over-month growth above 0.5%, signaling robust demand-side momentum that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 1,600 thousand units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,520 thousand and RSAFS at 639,829 million as of 2021-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where HOUST > 1,600k and RSAFS growth > 0.5%, or joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
